title: [HW Parameters] Improve .mlir syntax
body:
We're currently using a simple approach to printing and parsing parameter attributes, which means that we get things like this on an instance:

```
hw.module @UseParameters<xxx: i42>() {
  hw.instance "verbatimparam" @NoArg<param: i42 = #hw.param.binary<add #hw.param.verbatim<"xxx">, 17>>() -> () 
}
```

These parameters are only printed in specific contexts (right now: instances, localparams; in the future, in a `!hw.int<param>` sort of type), so we can do much better.  We should be able to get something like:

```
hw.module @UseParameters<xxx: i42>() {
  hw.instance "verbatimparam" @NoArg<param: i42 = xxx + 17>() -> () 
}
```

It may also be worth introducing a consistent sigil to make these things stand out more, and avoid potential keyword ambiguity.  SIL uses the τFoo prefix since it just deals with type parameters.  I think that using a backtick or something that is otherwise unused would make sense.  We would consistently print it in the module parameter list as well as at use-site, e.g. something like:
 
```
hw.module @UseParameters<`xxx: i42>() {
  hw.instance "verbatimparam" @NoArg<`param: i42 = `xxx + 17>() -> () 
```

Maybe ` isn't the best though.
